volumeTitle: Antigone. The Women of Trachis. Philoctetes. Oedipus at Colonus
label: Philoctetes (Section 907 - Section 1025)

sections.label: Section 907
sections.original: οὔκουν ἐν οἷς γε δρᾷς · ἐν οἷς δʼ αὐδᾷς ὀκνῶ .
sections.translation: Not because of your present deeds , at least . But because of your words , I worry .

sections.label: Section 908
sections.original: ὦ Ζεῦ , τί δράσω ; δεύτερον ληφθῶ κακός ,
sections.translation: O Zeus , what shall I do ? Must I be twice found base — by disloyal silence , as well as by shameful speech ?

sections.label: Section 910
sections.original: ἁνὴρ ὅδʼ , εἰ μὴ ʼγὼ κακὸς γνώμων ἔφυν ,
sections.translation: Unless I am lacking in judgment , he means to betray me , leave me behind and sail away !

sections.label: Section 912
sections.original: λιπὼν μὲν οὐκ ἔγωγε · λυπηρῶς δὲ μὴ
sections.translation: Leave you ? No , not I . Rather , to your pain , I will bring you along . That is my torment .

sections.label: Section 914
sections.original: τί ποτε λέγεις , ὦ τέκνον ; ὡς οὐ μανθάνω .
sections.translation: What do you mean , son ? I do not understand .

sections.label: Section 915
sections.original: οὐδέν σε κρύψω · δεῖ γὰρ ἐς Τροίαν σε πλεῖν
sections.translation: I will conceal nothing . You must sail to Troy , back to the Achaeans and the forces of the Atreids .

sections.label: Section 917
sections.original: οἴμοι , τί εἶπας ;
sections.translation: Ah , no ! What have you said ?

sections.label: Section 917a
sections.original: μὴ στέναζε , πρὶν μάθῃς .
sections.translation: Do not wail in grief , before you understand !

sections.label: Section 918
sections.original: ποῖον μάθημα ; τί με νοεῖς δρᾶσαί ποτε ;
sections.translation: Understand what ? What do you intend to do to me ?

sections.label: Section 919
sections.original: σῶσαι κακοῦ μὲν πρῶτα τοῦδʼ , ἔπειτα δὲ
sections.translation: Save you , first , from this misery , and then ,

sections.label: Section 920
sections.original: ξὺν σοὶ τὰ Τροίας πεδία πορθῆσαι μολών .
sections.translation: together with you , go and plunder Troy ’ s plains .

sections.label: Section 921
sections.original: καὶ ταῦτʼ ἀληθῆ δρᾶν νοεῖς ;
sections.translation: And this is your true intent ?

sections.label: Section 921a
sections.original: πολλὴ κρατεῖ
sections.translation: A harsh necessity governs these events , so do not be angered at hearing of them .

sections.label: Section 923
sections.original: ἀπόλωλα τλήμων , προδέδομαι . τί μʼ , ὦ ξένε ,
sections.translation: I am destroyed — ah , misery !— betrayed ! What have you done to me , stranger ? Return my bow at once !

sections.label: Section 925
sections.original: ἀλλʼ οὐχ οἷόν τε · τῶν γὰρ ἐν τέλει κλύειν
sections.translation: No , it is not possible . My duty and my interest alike constrain me to obey those in power .

sections.label: Section 927
sections.original: ὦ πῦρ σὺ καὶ πᾶν δεῖμα καὶ πανουργίας
sections.translation: You destroying fire , you utter monster , you hateful masterpiece of astounding wickedness ! What treachery you have done to me ! How thoroughly you have deceived me ! And are you , you wretch , unashamed to look at me ,

sections.label: Section 930
sections.original: τὸν προστρόπαιον , τὸν ἱκέτην , ὦ σχέτλιε ;
sections.translation: the suppliant who turned to you for mercy ? In taking my bow , you have robbed me of my life . Return it , I beg you , return it , I pray you , son ! By the gods of your fathers , do not rob me of my life ! Ah , me ! He speaks to me no more .

sections.label: Section 935
sections.original: ἀλλʼ ὡς μεθήσων μήποθʼ , ὧδʼ ὁρᾷ πάλιν .
sections.translation: He looks away , as if he will never give it up ! O you inlets and headlands , you wild creatures of the hills who have shared my life , and you jagged cliffs , to you — for you alone hear me — to you my accustomed companions ,

sections.label: Section 940
sections.original: οἷʼ ἔργʼ ὁ παῖς μʼ ἔδρασεν οὑξ Ἀχιλλέως ·
sections.translation: I bewail the treacherous treatment I have received from the son of Achilles . Although he swore to take me to my home , it is to Troy that he takes me . Although he gave me his right hand in pledge of his word , he has taken my bow , the sacred bow , once belonging to Zeus ’ s son Heracles , and he keeps it , and wants to show it to the Argives as his own .

sections.label: Section 945
sections.original: ὡς ἄνδρʼ ἑλὼν ἰσχυρόν ἐκ βίας μʼ ἄγει ,
sections.translation: By force he drags me away , as if he had captured a strong man , and does not see that he is cutting down a corpse , the shadow of smoke , a mere phantom . In my strength he could not have taken me — no !— nor even in my present condition , save by deceit . But now , because of my rotten fate , I have been tricked . What should I do ?

sections.label: Section 950
sections.original: ἀλλʼ ἀπόδος , ἀλλὰ νῦν ἔτʼ ἐν σαυτῷ γενοῦ .
sections.translation: Wait , give it back ! Now , at least , recover your true self ! What do you say ? Silence ! I am nothing ! Double-gated cave , back , back again I return to you , but now stripped and lacking the means to live . Yes , in that chamber I will wither away alone ,

sections.label: Section 955
sections.original: οὐ πτηνὸν ὄρνιν οὐδὲ θῆρʼ ὀρειβάτην
sections.translation: bringing down with that bow no winged bird , no beast that roams the hills . Rather I myself shall die in misery , and supply a feast for those who fed me , becoming the prey of those on whom I preyed . Ah , in requital for blood my own blood will flow ,

sections.label: Section 960
sections.original: πρὸς τοῦ δοκοῦντος οὐδὲν εἰδέναι κακόν .
sections.translation: shed at the hands of a man who seemed all unknowing of evil ! May destruction seize you !— but no , not yet , not before I learn whether you will change your mind . But if not , may you die a cruel death !

sections.label: Section 963
sections.original: τί δρῶμεν ; ἐν σοὶ καὶ τὸ πλεῖν ἡμᾶς , ἄναξ ,
sections.translation: What shall we do ? It rests with you , my king , whether we now sail , or move forward to answer his pleas .

sections.label: Section 965
sections.original: ἐμοὶ μὲν οἶκτος δεινὸς ἐμπέπτωκέ τις
sections.translation: A startling pity for him has come upon me — and not now for the first time , but long ago .

sections.label: Section 967
sections.original: ἐλέησον , ὦ παῖ , πρὸς θεῶν , καὶ μὴ παρῇς
sections.translation: Show mercy , boy , for the love of the gods , and do not give men cause to reproach you for having cheated me .

sections.label: Section 969
sections.original: οἴμοι , τί δράσω ; μή ποτʼ ὤφελον λιπεῖν
sections.translation: Ah , no , what shall I do ? I wish I had never left

sections.label: Section 970
sections.original: τὴν Σκῦρον · οὕτω τοῖς παροῦσιν ἄχθομαι .
sections.translation: Scyros , so pained am I by these doings .

sections.label: Section 971
sections.original: οὐκ εἶ κακὸς σύ , πρὸς κακῶν δʼ ἀνδρῶν μαθὼν
sections.translation: You are not in and of yourself wicked , but you seem to have come to me after learning the shameless lessons of wicked masters . Now leave such behavior to others , whom it suits , and sail from here — once you have given me back my weapons .

sections.label: Section 974
sections.original: τί δρῶμεν , ἄνδρες ;
sections.translation: What shall we do , friends ?

sections.label: Section 975
sections.original: οὐκ εἶ μεθεὶς τὰ τόξα ταῦτʼ ἐμοὶ πάλιν ;
sections.translation: Traitor , what are you doing ? Come back here and surrender that bow to me !

sections.label: Section 976
sections.original: οἴμοι , τίς ἁνήρ ; ἆρʼ Ὀδυσσέως κλύω ;
sections.translation: Who is that ? Do I hear Odysseus ?

sections.label: Section 977
sections.original: Ὀδυσσέως , σάφʼ ἴσθʼ , ἐμοῦ γʼ , ὃν εἰσορᾷς .
sections.translation: Yes , Odysseus , be sure of it . Here I am before your eyes .

sections.label: Section 978
sections.original: οἴμοι · πέπραμαι κἀπόλωλʼ · ὅδʼ ἦν ἄρα
sections.translation: Ah , me , I am sold , destroyed ! It was he , then , who entrapped me and robbed me of my arms .

sections.label: Section 980
sections.original: ἐγώ , σάφʼ ἴσθʼ , οὐκ ἄλλος · ὁμολογῶ τάδε .
sections.translation: Yes , I and no other . I avow it .

sections.label: Section 981
sections.original: ἀπόδος , ἄφες μοι , παῖ , τὰ τόξα .
sections.translation: Give back my bow , boy , give it up .

sections.label: Section 981a
sections.original: τοῦτο μέν ,
sections.translation: That he shall never do , even if he wished to . And , what is more , you must come along with it , or my men will bring you by force .

sections.label: Section 984
sections.original: ἔμʼ , ὦ κακῶν κάκιστε καὶ τολμήστατε ,
sections.translation: Me , you basest and boldest of scoundrels ,

sections.label: Section 985
sections.original: οἵδʼ ἐκ βίας ἄξουσιν ;
sections.translation: they will take me by force ?

sections.label: Section 985a
sections.original: ἢν μὴ ἕρπῃς ἑκών .
sections.translation: Unless you come of your own free will .

sections.label: Section 986
sections.original: ὦ Λημνία χθὼν καὶ τὸ παγκρατὲς σέλας
sections.translation: O Lemnos , and you all-conquering flame kindled by Hephaestus , will you indeed endure it that this man should take me from your domain by force ?

sections.label: Section 989
sections.original: Ζεύς ἐσθʼ , ἵνʼ εἰδῇς , Ζεύς , ὁ τῆσδε γῆς κρατῶν ,
sections.translation: Zeus it is , I tell you , Zeus , who rules this land ,

sections.label: Section 990
sections.original: Ζεύς , ᾧ δέδοκται ταῦθʼ · ὑπηρετῶ δʼ ἐγώ .
sections.translation: and it is by Zeus that these actions are decreed . I am his servant .

sections.label: Section 991
sections.original: ὦ μῖσος , οἷα κἀξανευρίσκεις λέγειν ·
sections.translation: Hated creature , what clever pleading you devise ! By sheltering yourself behind the gods , you make the gods liars .

sections.label: Section 993
sections.original: οὔκ , ἀλλʼ ἀληθεῖς · ἡ δʼ ὁδὸς πορευτέα .
sections.translation: No , but true prophets . Now our march must begin .

sections.label: Section 994
sections.original: οὔ φημʼ .
sections.translation: Never !

sections.label: Section 994a
sections.original: ἐγὼ δέ φημι . πειστέον τάδε .
sections.translation: Now , I say . You must obey .

sections.label: Section 995
sections.original: οἴμοι τάλας . ἡμᾶς μὲν ὡς δούλους σαφῶς
sections.translation: Ah , misery ! Clearly , then , my father sired me to be a slave and no free man .

sections.label: Section 997
sections.original: οὔκ , ἀλλʼ ὁμοίους τοῖς ἀρίστοισιν , μεθʼ ὧν
sections.translation: Not so , but to be the peer of the best and bravest , with whom you are destined to take Troy and force it to the ground .

sections.label: Section 999
sections.original: οὐδέποτέ γʼ · οὐδʼ ἢν χρῇ με πᾶν παθεῖν κακόν ,
sections.translation: No , never — even if I must suffer every torment ,

sections.label: Section 1000
sections.original: ἕως ἂν ᾖ μοι γῆς τόδʼ αἰπεινὸν βάθρον .
sections.translation: so long as I have this island ’ s steep cliffs beneath me !

sections.label: Section 1001
sections.original: τί δʼ ἐργασείεις ;
sections.translation: What do you plan to do ?

sections.label: Section 1001a
sections.original: κρᾶτʼ ἐμὸν τόδʼ αὐτίκα
sections.translation: Throw myself now from the rock and shatter my head on the rocks below !

sections.label: Section 1003
sections.original: ξυλλάβετον αὐτόν · μὴ ʼπὶ τῷδʼ ἔστω τάδε .
sections.translation: Quick , seize him , both of you ! Do not give him the chance !

sections.label: Section 1004
sections.original: ὦ χεῖρες , οἷα πάσχετʼ ἐν χρείᾳ φίλης
sections.translation: O my arms , what shames you suffer for lack of your cherished

sections.label: Section 1005
sections.original: νευρᾶς , ὑπʼ ἀνδρὸς τοῦδε συνθηρώμεναι .
sections.translation: bow , now that you are made that man ’ s bound quarry ! And you , who cannot think one healthy or one noble thought , how stealthily you have once more ambushed and trapped me , taking this boy for your screen , because he was a stranger to me . He is too good for your company , but worthy of mine ,

sections.label: Section 1010
sections.original: ὃς οὐδὲν ᾔδει πλὴν τὸ προσταχθὲν ποεῖν ,
sections.translation: since he had no thought but to execute his orders , and he already shows remorse for his own errors and for the wrongs done me . But your corrupt mind , always on the lookout from some position of ambush , trained him well — unsuited and unwilling though he was —

sections.label: Section 1015
sections.original: εὖ προυδίδαξεν ἐν κακοῖς εἶναι σοφόν .
sections.translation: to be cunning in evil . And now , wretch , for me you plan bonds and passage from the very shore on which you had me flung away , friendless , abandoned , citiless , a corpse in the eyes of the living . Ah ! Hades take you ! Indeed I have often so prayed for you .

sections.label: Section 1020
sections.original: ἀλλʼ οὐ γὰρ οὐδὲν θεοὶ νέμουσιν ἡδύ μοι ,
sections.translation: But , because the gods grant me nothing that is sweet , you remain alive and you laugh , while I live miserably among countless sufferings , mocked by you and by the sons of Atreus , the two generals whom you serve on this errand .

sections.label: Section 1025
sections.original: καίτοι σὺ μὲν κλοπῇ τε κἀνάγκῃ ζυγεὶς
sections.translation: And yet you sailed with them only when brought under their yoke by trickery and compulsion . But me , when , to my utter ruin , I sailed of my own accord as their mate with seven ships , me they cast out of ship and honor , as you claim , while they say that it was your doing . And now , why would you take me ? Why have me led away ? For what purpose ?
